atoms.source_snippet: Concrete invariant: executor capability equality gate in ritual_async (payload) ties precompile address to registry enum.
atoms.semantic_claim: TEE registry alignment: for each live async job enabled in the v2.2 FSM, builder/verifier resolve the executor via `read_executor` / `Capability` and enforce `executor.capability == registry_capability_for_precompile(precompile_address)` from `ritual-precompile-addresses` metadata; this live gate must match traffic-gen executor selection and node CAPABILITIES. Multimodal/FHE capability metadata is covered by file-0316 atoms, but their Phase 2 delivery is not green until pricing/model-registry parity is ported.

destinations.evidence: Payload/async path compares TEE-registered executor capability to metadata-derived expectation per precompile.
Canonical expected capability tag for each precompile; cross-repo proof in verify_async_precompile_cross_repo.py.
Static check binds Reth metadata to traffic-gen Capability strings and docker.py wiring.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Executor capability gating is implemented in ritual_async and sourced from ritual-precompile-addresses for live enabled async jobs; cross-repo drift is caught by verify_async_precompile_cross_repo.py and file-0316 contract atoms. This atom no longer claims that all registered two-phase precompiles have complete runtime delivery parity.

atoms.semantic_claim: Payload building classifies heartbeat, obligations, async simulations, commitments, fulfilled async, delivery, sequencing rights, regular transactions, and diagnostics under one deadline and demotion policy.
atoms.required_change: No code change in this v2 slice; behavior is intentionally inactive or out of scope.

atoms.notes: Sequencing-rights classification/reordering is not active in the current v2.2 slice. Payload building keeps upstream tip ordering plus scheduled/async obligation handling, so old sequencing-rights crate/API atoms are intentionally absent.

atoms.semantic_claim: Heartbeat revival runs as a payload-builder phase that hydrates tracker state from contract storage on cold start and only injects checkAndRevive work when expired agents exist.
atoms.required_change: No code change in this v2 slice; behavior is intentionally inactive or out of scope.

atoms.notes: Heartbeat revival payload injection is not active in the v2.2 scheduled/async slice; the live scheduled FSM is based on Scheduler obligations, not heartbeat revival lanes.

atoms.semantic_claim: Fulfilled async processing batch-reads SPC verification data before per-transaction processing and falls back to per-tx verification only if the batch read fails.

atoms.semantic_claim: Phase 2 delivery collection reads each delivery-ready job from AsyncJobTracker, defers jobs whose Phase 1 is not settled, and skips missing on-chain jobs instead of trusting pool state alone.

destinations.evidence: builder gathers delivery-ready pool entries and skips entries whose preparation fails
checks AsyncJobTracker phase1-settled state

atoms.equivalence_argument: Phase 2 delivery collection is preserved by combining pool delivery-ready entries with contract-state checks that skip non-settled or missing jobs.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Replay the smallest Long-Running Two-Phase Trace scenario that exercises this source-owned behavior and fail if the v2.2 port omits or changes it.
atoms.notes: Mapped to builder loop plus preparation guard.

atoms.semantic_claim: Phase 2 delivery settlement checks pool metadata against contract state for precompile input hash, commit block, and precompile address before constructing a delivery transaction.

destinations.evidence: validates pool metadata against contract precompile, commit block, input size/hash, and origin RLP

atoms.equivalence_argument: Delivery-settlement builder preserves pool-vs-contract checks for precompile address, commit block, input size, input hash, and origin tx RLP hash.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Replay the smallest Long-Running Two-Phase Trace scenario that exercises this source-owned behavior and fail if the v2.2 port omits or changes it.
atoms.notes: Active guard behavior preserved.

atoms.semantic_claim: Phase 2 delivery expiry uses the contract expiry block, computed as max commit block plus TTL or Phase 2 deadline, so proposer-side inclusion cannot diverge from verifier-side contract checks.

destinations.evidence: pool uses and updates contract_expiry_block
payload checks current_block against contract expiry_block

atoms.equivalence_argument: Phase 2 delivery expiry is preserved by pool-side eligibility and payload-side contract expiry validation.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Replay the smallest Long-Running Two-Phase Trace scenario that exercises this source-owned behavior and fail if the v2.2 port omits or changes it.
atoms.notes: Builder and pool use contract-derived expiry boundaries.

atoms.semantic_claim: Phase 2 delivery settlement construction verifies a delivery result exists, packs ZK result and proof together for ZK jobs, extracts the TEE address from the Phase 1 commitment, and creates the system settlement from contract job data.

destinations.evidence: requires delivery result/proof, validates SPC context, packs ZK result+proof, and creates system settlement

atoms.equivalence_argument: Phase 2 delivery construction is preserved for active HTTP/ZK paths.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Replay the smallest Long-Running Two-Phase Trace, zk_two_phase scenario that exercises this source-owned behavior and fail if the v2.2 port omits or changes it.
atoms.notes: Active payload behavior preserved.

atoms.semantic_claim: Phase 2 delivery pricing branches by precompile: sovereign agents charge work-based fees, media and FHE charge result-derived fees or DA-error fees, persistent agents charge spawn fees, and HTTP/ZK use block-based pricing.

destinations.snippet: sovereign/persistent/HTTP/ZK pricing is present; media/FHE old result-derived pricing is not ported
destinations.evidence: Current code supports long-HTTP, ZK, sovereign work-metric pricing, and persistent spawn fees; it does not implement old image/audio/video model-specific result pricing or FHE output-size pricing.
Payload/verifier fail-close unported media/FHE delivery families rather than accepting generic shared pricing.

atoms.required_change: Split and port old multimodal/FHE Phase 2 pricing before marking this broad pricing branch atom green. Keep current fail-closed guards for unported families.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Replay the smallest Long-Running Two-Phase Trace, Product Economics Compatibility, Multimodal Precompile Trace scenario that exercises this source-owned behavior and fail if the v2.2 port omits or changes it.
atoms.notes: Re-audited from first principles: previous dirty atom falsely claimed all registered two-phase precompiles were enabled through shared pricing. That would miss media/FHE old-FSM pricing differences. Supported-family pricing is covered by narrower atoms; this broad media/FHE-inclusive atom remains blocked.

atoms.semantic_claim: Phase 2 delivery settlement encodes an AsyncDelivery.deliver call and pays only the executor fee, with validator fees set to zero because Phase 1 already paid validators.

destinations.evidence: encodes DELIVER_SELECTOR and creates TxAsyncSettlement with validator fees set to zero

atoms.equivalence_argument: Phase 2 delivery settlement calldata and fee assignment are preserved for the active delivery path.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Replay the smallest Long-Running Two-Phase Trace scenario that exercises this source-owned behavior and fail if the v2.2 port omits or changes it.
atoms.notes: Validator fees are zero in Phase 2 because Phase 1 already paid validators.

atoms.source_hunk_hint: split from file-0092-atom-0008: model registry / ModelCache
atoms.source_snippet: prepare_fulfilled_async_settlement and related paths use ritual_async_fees::get_model_registry_address, ModelCache
atoms.semantic_claim: Model registry alignment: live settlement/pricing paths read model-registry-backed pricing through `get_model_registry_address` and per-block `ModelCache` where old FSM pricing requires model data (LLM today). Multimodal model-registry pricing remains unresolved because v2.2 does not yet port old image/audio/video Phase 2 model-specific pricing.

destinations.evidence: Fulfilled async settlement takes ModelCache and queries model registry address from ritual_async_fees.
Per-block model cache constructed in default payload builder path.

atoms.equivalence_argument: ModelCache threading and get_model_registry_address usage are present for live LLM/short settlement pricing. The previous broad model-registry claim is narrowed: image/audio/video model-specific Phase 2 pricing is not considered green and must be ported before enabling those delivery families.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: cargo test -p reth-ethereum-payload-builder --locked
atoms.notes: First-principles audit found old media Phase 2 pricing depends on model-registry fields that the current v2 reader still zeroes/omits. Do not use this atom to justify enabling image/audio/video delivery.

atoms.source_hunk_hint: split from file-0092-atom-0008: wallet locks / verify_wallet_for_commitment
atoms.source_snippet: ritual_async_fees::verify_wallet_for_commitment used in commitment/settlement validation
atoms.semantic_claim: Wallet lock alignment: async commitment validation calls `verify_wallet_for_commitment` so live two-phase families include old-FSM executor/escrow assumptions, including sovereign worst-case iteration/tool-call escrow and Phase 2 callback gas/value cost for long-HTTP, ZK, sovereign, and persistent agent jobs.

destinations.evidence: Async payload path imports and invokes wallet verification for commitments.

destinations.snippet: verify_wallet_for_commitment includes callback cost and sovereign max work escrow
destinations.evidence: Wallet verification now decodes Phase 2 callback gas/value for enabled two-phase precompiles and uses sovereign max iteration/tool-call escrow instead of generic phase-1-only fees.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Wallet verification is wired through payload/verifier commitment checks and now preserves the old supported-family fee/lock shape for long-HTTP, ZK, sovereign, and persistent two-phase jobs. Media/FHE wallet/model parity remains blocked with their delivery runtime parity.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: cargo test -p ritual-async-fees --locked; cargo test -p reth-ethereum-payload-builder --locked ritual_async
atoms.notes: Narrowed to live supported families. This atom should fail review if sovereign uses generic two-phase phase-1 escrow or if callback gas/value is omitted from two-phase wallet lock requirements.

summary: First-principles async pass narrowed broad payload atoms and blocked media/FHE-inclusive Phase 2 pricing until old result/model pricing is ported.
notes: First-principles async pass: active long-HTTP/ZK/sovereign/persistent delivery paths mapped; multimodal/FHE runtime delivery remains blocked until old model/FHE pricing and result decoding are ported. Existing broad structural-equivalent claims were narrowed to live supported families.
